message:
fix(session): clone entries during full rewrite instead of mem::take to prevent data loss on async drop

The full-rewrite path in Session::save() previously used
std::mem::take(&mut self.entries) to move entries into the background
thread, leaving the session with an empty entries vector. If the async
future was dropped before completion (e.g. timeout, cancellation), the
entries would be permanently lost from the in-memory session state.

Changes:
- Replace std::mem::take with self.entries.clone() so the session
  retains its entries throughout the save operation
- On success: ignore the returned entries (session already has them),
  just update persisted_entry_count and clear checkpoint counter
- On error: ignore the returned entries from the error tuple (session
  still has its original entries intact), propagate only the error
- Update crash_entries_survive_failed_full_rewrite test comments to
  reflect the new clone-based safety model

The clone has negligible overhead relative to the IO-bound save
operation, and eliminates the window where entries exist only in the
background thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
